======= RMA00768Everett Franklin Phillips papers21-23-768Compiled by M.E. WarrenDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections

converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Everett Franklin Phillips papers
Phillips, Everett Franklin, 1878-1951.
21-23-768
4.3 cubic feet. 4.3 cubic feet.
1868-1950. Correspondence, reports, papers. Correspondence, reports, and other papers relating to bees and beekeeping. Collection material in English 2098517
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
M.E. Warren
Date completed:
March 1990
EAD encoding:
Ann Hubert, June 2004
Date modified:
RMC Staff, May 2017
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Professor of Apiculture, Cornell University.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Correspondence, reports, and other papers relating to bees and beekeeping. Includes material on the manufacture, patent assignment, alleged patent infringement, and use of royalties of the product made by the Dyce Honey Process; on Nellie J. Stratton's development of a honey and lactose candy; on the coordination of entomologists' research efforts during World War II; on the establishment and growth of the Charles C. Miller Memorial Library; and of the publication of beekeeping journals, pamphlets, and books.
Additional papers include correspondence with other apiologists and entomologists concerning the behavior and raising of bees and improved methods for producing and shipping honey, with the A.I. Root Company of Medina, Ohio, producers of beekeepers' supplies and honey and publishers of Gleanings in Bee Culture, with editors of the American Bee Journal, and with U.S. Representatives Gilbert N. Haugen, Franklin Menges, William L. Nelson, and Gale H. Stalker and Senators Robert F. Wagner, Sr., concerning the opposition of American honey interests to bills (1928, 1930) designed to allow the addition of corn sugars to manufactured products without marking this addition on the label; together with an unpublished ms. by Phillips on the behavior of bees and photograph concerning beekeeping.
Also includes correspondence, 1868-1888; petition relating to adulteration of honey, 1878, and one Japanese fable presented by Marice G. Dadant to Dr. Phillips, 1948, selected by C. P. Dadant and Charles Dadant, previously located in Mann Library, Cornell University.
Correspondents include Charles P. Alexander, Percy N. Annand, Liberty Hyde Bailey, Camille Pierre Dadant, Elton J. Dyce, Livingston Farrand,Albert R. Mann, James G. Needham, Charles E. Palm, Sherman Peer, Franklin D. Roosevelt, Ernest H. Root, Huber H. Root, Nellie J. Stratton, Henry A. Wallace, Sherman Whitney, and many others.
Cite As:
Everett Franklin Phillips papers, #21-23-768. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Patents. Dyce Honey Process. Honey -- Law and legislation. Honey. Entomology -- Study and teaching. Entomology. Entomologists. Candy. Beekeepers. Bee culture -- Study and teaching. Bee culture. Agriculture -- Law and legislation. Alexander, Charles P. (Charles Paul), 1889-1981. Annand, Percy N. Bailey, L. H. (Liberty Hyde), 1858-1954. Dadant, C. P. (Camille Pierre), 1851-1938. Dadant, Charles. Dyce, Elton J. Farrand, Livingston, 1867-1939. Mann, Albert Russell, 1880-1947. Needham, James G. (James George), 1868-1957. Palm, Charles Edmund, 1911-1996. Peer, Sherman. Roosevelt, Franklin D. (Franklin Delano), 1882-1945. Root, Ernest H. Root, H. H. (Huber H.) Stratton, Nellie J. Wallace, Henry A. Whitney, Sherman. Charles C. Miller Memorial Library Cornell University -- : Faculty. New York State College of Agriculture. Department of Entomology

Root Co.)1926-193438Snodgras, R.E.1924-193139Stewart, Charles - European foulbrood1925310Sturges, Arthur P.1924-1931311Sturtevant, Arnold P.1924-1931312Vaillancourt, C.1924-1931313Watson, Lloyd R.1924-1933314Wilson, H.F.1924-1926315Miscellaneous correspondence1939-1947316Papers and reportsMiscellaneous documents pertaining to the drafting and enactment of the law to regulate importation of adult honeybees into the United States1922317"History of fight against corn sugar interests in the United States"1926-1930318-20Office of Defense Transportation and Office of Price Administration1942-1944321War Production Board circulars1942-1944322USDA leaflets1938323Canada - Wartime Prices and Trade Board1942-1944324Zoology as an Applied Science1911325Temperature and Wintering of Bees1914326The Present Opportunity in Beekeeping1918327Recent Investigations on Bee Diseases1924328Papers on persons concerned with bees and beekeeping: Aristotle, presented by Paul Needham; Johannes Dzierson, presented by Everett Oertel; F.W.L. Sladen, presented by E.F. Phillips; A.J. Cook, presented by R.B. Wilsob; G.M. Doolittle, presented by A.C. Gould; John S. Harabison, presented by R.L. Parker1925-1926329Frank C. Pellett, naturalist: brochure (with picture) on lectures given by him on Chautauqua Circuit330Julius Robert Hoffman, commercial beekeeper: biographical sketch1925331Program for first short course in beekeeping, Cornell University1919332The Contact of Apiculture with the Basic Sciences1929333Information on effect of altitude on nectar secretion by Phillips1920334Reports on Occurrence of Brood Diseases of Bees and Activities in Inspection Work1912335Correspondence with R.F. Remer, Sioux Honey Assn., Sioux City, Iowa, concerning: manufacture of honey under Dyce process by Sioux Honey Assn.; problems of cooperatives for handling honey; release of honey from price regulations. Also correspondence with E.J. Dyce, W.J. Dedicott, S.F. Wadden, Allan H. Treman, G.F. Rogalsky1940-1950336Correspondence on patent infringements on Dyce Process with Lloyd B. Sherman, V.V. Dexter, Sherman Whitney, C.E. Hamann. Comparison of Lund and Dyce honey processes. Copy of contract with Honey Butter Products Corp. Also correspondence with J.J.C. Downey, G.F. Rogalsky, D.B. Bradshaw, Sherman Peer, L.N. Simmons, Seth J. Shaw337Nellie J. Stratton1928-1946338-40
Scope and Contents
Correspondence and reports re development of a honey and lactose candy by Miss Stratton. Mr. James Collins, Blue Moon Cheese Products Co., Messrs. S.F. Whitman and Sons, W. E. Bliss, Pres. The Honey Candy Co. of America, L.A. Beeghley (re patents), Karl Fenning (re Patent), S. Erison, The Honey Candy Co. of America, Fred C. Taylor, The Sugar Institute, Inc., W. Guthrie, Truscon Steel Co., H.P. Weld, Dept. of Psychology, Cornell UniversityMr. Rapheal Gould, American Library Service: re Frans Blom and Edwin C. Alphonsus ms. on beekeeping in Maya civilization341Questions based on A.B. Comstock's book "How to Keep Bees" for use in correspondence course given by Dr. Burton N. Gates, Massachusettes Ag. College. Also questions based on Phillips' book "Beekeeping"342Correspondence re obtaining material on John S. Harbison: Mrs. M.J. Harbison, Mrs. John S. Harbison, Mrs. John C. Taggart, Charles B. Phillips343Letter to Col. Charles A. Lindberg re song written in his honor. Correspondence with M. Charles Louis Loretan re song on beekeeping - words and music included1926343Franklin D. Roosevelt re honey sent to him343John W. Moir, Edinburgh, Scotland re exchanges with Cornell Library of material on beekeeping344K.Y. Fay re Chinese beekeeping literature and material he supplied to beekeeping library1925-1926345Imogene B. Woloctt (Mrs. Roger Wilcott) re proposed book on life of Charles Dadant345Outline for courses in beekeeping, North Carolina State College, made by J.E. Eckert345C.B. Williams, letter re Braula Coeca in England345Ing. Ctibor Blattny, Czechoslovakia - Correspondence and brief abstract of his work on artificial rearing of mites1928345Arthur C. Miller re publication of pamphlet "The Wiley Honey Life"1906345Florence Naille, correspondence re preparation of biography of L.L. Langstroth. Mrs. H.R. Philbrick, Rev. Frederick B. Noss, E.R. Root347-48G.A. Rosch, correspondence beginning in 1927; letter from Frau Brubrun Rosch re fate of her husband1927349Dr. L. Bahr re formation of an international society of bee-culture41O.E. Gallup, correspondence re Elisha Gallup42Mrs. W.Z. Hutchinson re books and a portrait presented to her by the beekeeping library42Mrs. W.Z. Lloyd re Peabody Honey Extractor donated to Cornell University42G.P. Putnam (publisher), letter re Gilman book "Practical Bee Breeding"42Longmans, Green and Co. (publisher), letters re E.B. Wednore's book "A Manual of Beekeeping"193243N.G. Eremie re plan for adopting a standard bee hive for Romania192643Arthur C. Miller, correspondence re his beekeeping library and book plate for Charles C. Miller library43James G. Needham re work in beekeeping at Cornell University43C.C. Miller Memorial Library: its establishment and growth. C.P. Dadant, Chairman of Committee on Miller Memorial Library, J.G. Needham, H.G. Rowe, Gleanings in Bee Culture, E.R. Root, Mrs. Sidney J. Miller, E.G. Lestourgeon, Mgr. Texas Honey Producers Assn., Louis H. School, The Beekeepers' Item, H.F. Wilson, S.W. Beyer1920-3044War Effort of Entomologists194344-7
Scope and Contents
Letters and reports of Committee on Coordination of Entomology with the War Effort; report on Conference on War Problems in Biology and Agriculture; report on War Activities of Bureau of Entomology and Plant Quarantine; U.S. Dept. of Ag. Report on Ag. Production of 1943.
Correspondence with the following: P.N. Annand, Chief Bureau of Ent., Washington, D.C.; Robert Griggs; C.E. Palm; Dept. of Entomology, Cornell University; Harry B. Weiss, President Am. Assn. Econ. Ent.; F.L. Campbell; C.H. Richardson; Charles P. Alexander, Pres. Ent. Soc. of Am.; F.C. Bishop, Chief Joint Committee on War-Work Training; C.E. Mickel, Ent. Soc. of America; E.O. Essig, professor of Entomology, Univ. of California; John S. Hauser; W.B. Herms, Univ. of California; Ernest N. Cory; H.A. Wallace, Sec., Dept. of Ag.; H.B. Hungerford, Univ. of Kansas; Harold D. Smith, Dir., Bureau of Budget; Avery S. Hoyt, U.S. Dept. of Ag.Correspondence re honey grading - H.W. Samson, U.S. Bureau of Ag. Econ., E.P. Norgord, Dept. of Farms and Markets, Albany48Crop Protection Committee reports and correspondenceFeb. 13-14, 194349
Scope and Contents
Correspondence with E.C. Stackman, Chairman; Robert F. Griggs, Chairman Div. of Biol. and Ag., Natl. Research Council; Major Gen. Lewis B. Hershey; Dr. J.G. Leach, Acting Chairman, War Emer. Committee; Harry B. Weiss; J.D. Walker, Chairman of Sub-committee on Seed Certification; Ross G. Harrison, Chairman, Natl. Research Council. Also, correspondence with Dr. Walter F. Loehwing and G.W. Keitt, Prof. of Plant Pathology, Univ. of Wisconsin
Mimeo entitled "A Proposal re Ag. Services in Foreign Territories Occupied by American Troops."
Report on meeting of Am. Phytopathological Society, Feb. 13-14, 1943. Report of Fungicide Sub-committee, War Emergency Committee, Am. Phytopathological Society. Memo on the War Problem of Manpower in Plant PathologyCorrespondence, review, and contract on W.F. Phillips' book "Beekeeping." L.H. Bailey, H.S. Latham, The Macmillan Co., E.A. Elder410Lloyd R. Watson, data on variation in tongue length of progeny of queenbees1937411Correspondence re patent assignment of Dyce Process to Cornell University; use of royalties; honey prices; building of apiary; complaints of liscenses. Elton J. Dyce, G.F. Rogalsky, Livingston Farrand, A.R. Mann411Canadian patent in Dyce honey process; license agreement. J.B. Fairbairn, Deputy Min. of Ag., F.E. Millen, Ontario Ag. College, E.J. Dyce, Robert V. Morse, M.B. Gordon, Smart and Biggar412Letters re review of Maeterlinck book reportedly by Mark Twain. Albert Bigelow Paine413Letter from C.H. Lake and biog. note on him413Coordination of entomology with war effort; occupational classification bulletin. Correspondence with Ernest N. Cory, A.G. Ruggles, Dr. Leonard Carmichael414Reports and correspondence re activities of the special Committee on Entomological Work in the Dept. of Ag. and in the States (to consider impending reorganization of Dept. of Ag.) Ag. Appropiation. Dr. Lewis W. Douglass1934415Interference of C.J. Bangham in securing patent on Dyce Process. Charles Bangham, R.A. Emerson, L.N. Simmons, E.J. Dyce, Robert V. Morse, J.B. Fairbairn51Honey bottling business with Preserves and Honey, Inc.; honey experiments. E.R. Root, Huber H. Root51Correspondence re American Bee Journal's claim of priority on Dyce Process. M.G. Dandant, Am. Bee Journal, E.J. Dyce52Correspondence re Dyce Process patent and library of beekeeping literature at Cornell. R.F. Remer, Robert V. Morse, L.N. Simmons, Livingston Farrand, G.F. Rogalsky, E.J. Dyce53Correspondence re misunderstanding that arose over similarity in Dyce and Latham honey process. Allen Latham54F. Eric Miller re his hoped for graduate work, job possibilities55George W. Adams, letter re his beekeeping experiences55John H. Lovell re his book on honey plants and abstracting of honey plant literature55Cornell Research Foundation vs. Sioux Honey Assn. Allan H. Treman, G.F. Rogalsky56M. l'Abbe A. Delaigues, request to bequeath his library to Cornell57Feldmarscaaleutnant Alexander von Matyas re E.F. Phillips visit to Hungry57Dr, Hugo von Buttle-Reepen re translation of his pamphlet "Sin Bienen Reflexmachinen," by Mary Geisler, and financial aid given to him58Alexander Bogdanoff re book on bee-hives and subject of bee-hives58F.W.L. Sladen, author of The Humble Bee, a book on queen rearing and other papers59Correspondence re application for British patent on Dyce Process and request for its revocation by New Zealand Honey Control Board. E.J. Dyce and Latham honey process. Allen Latham510Superior Honey Co. application for license to manufacture honey by Dyce method; price ceilings. M.S. Stone, Superior Honey Co., L.N. Simmons, S.D. Redfield, Superior Honey Co., E.J. Dyce, Don H. Lee511Samuel Cushman, letter requesting copies of his publications512Confidential letter to editors of all bee journals re editorial policy of bee journals512Other materialPhotographs (none of Dyce Lab)513-18The Bee Man: Life and Letters of Everett Franklin Phillips by Mary Geisler Phillips1967519Letter from L.L. Langstroth to "Ernest" and notes to Mr. Malin's letter to Langstroth1895520Miscellany, including memos from 1904-1907 for which Mary Phillips used the blank side for translating beekeeping articles from German to English521LettersD.L. Bradshaw, Sherman Peer, L.N. Simmons, Seth J. Shaw337Nellie J. Stratton1928-1946338-40
Scope and Contents
Correspondence and reports re development of a honey and lactose candy by Miss Stratton. Mr. James Collins, Blue Moon Cheese Products Co., Messrs. S.F. Whitman and Sons, W. E. Bliss, Pres. The Honey Candy Co. of America, L.A. Beeghley (re patents), Karl Fenning (re Patent), S. Erison, The Honey Candy Co. of America, Fred C. Taylor, The Sugar Institute, Inc., W. Guthrie, Truscon Steel Co., H.P. Weld, Dept. of Psychology, Cornell UniversityMr. Rapheal Gould, American Library Service: re Frans Blom and Edwin C. Alphonsus ms. on beekeeping in Maya civilization341Questions based on A.B. Comstock's book "How to Keep Bees" for use in correspondence course given by Dr. Burton N. Gates, Massachusettes Ag. College. Also questions based on Phillips' book "Beekeeping"342Correspondence re obtaining material on John S. Harbison: Mrs. M.J. Harbison, Mrs. John S. Harbison, Mrs. John C. Taggart, Charles B. Phillips343Letter to Col. Charles A. Lindberg re song written in his honor. Correspondence with M. Charles Louis Loretan re song on beekeeping - words and music included1926343Franklin D. Roosevelt re honey sent to him343John W. Moir, Edinburgh, Scotland re exchanges with Cornell Library of material on beekeeping344K.Y. Fay re Chinese beekeeping literature and material he supplied to beekeeping library1925-1926345Imogene B. Woloctt (Mrs. Roger Wilcott) re proposed book on life of Charles Dadant345Outline for courses in beekeeping, North Carolina State College, made by J.E. Eckert345C.B. Williams, letter re Braula Coeca in England345Ing. Ctibor Blattny, Czechoslovakia - Correspondence and brief abstract of his work on artificial rearing of mites1928345Arthur C. Miller re publication of pamphlet "The Wiley Honey Life"1906345Florence Naille, correspondence re preparation of biography of L.L. Langstroth. Mrs. H.R. Philbrick, Rev. Frederick B. Noss, E.R. Root347-48G.A. Rosch, correspondence beginning in 1927; letter from Frau Brubrun Rosch re fate of her husband1927349Dr. L. Bahr re formation of an international society of bee-culture41O.E. Gallup, correspondence re Elisha Gallup42Mrs. W.Z. Hutchinson re books and a portrait presented to her by the beekeeping library42Mrs. W.Z. Lloyd re Peabody Honey Extractor donated to Cornell University42G.P. Putnam (publisher), letter re Gilman book "Practical Bee Breeding"42Longmans, Green and Co. (publisher), letters re E.B. Wednore's book "A Manual of Beekeeping"193243N.G. Eremie re plan for adopting a standard bee hive for Romania192643Arthur C. Miller, correspondence re his beekeeping library and book plate for Charles C. Miller library43James G. Needham re work in beekeeping at Cornell University43C.C. Miller Memorial Library: its establishment and growth. C.P. Dadant, Chairman of Committee on Miller Memorial Library, J.G. Needham, H.G. Rowe, Gleanings in Bee Culture, E.R. Root, Mrs. Sidney J. Miller, E.G. Lestourgeon, Mgr. Texas Honey Producers Assn., Louis H. School, The Beekeepers' Item, H.F. Wilson, S.W. Beyer1920-3044War Effort of Entomologists194344-7
Scope and Contents
Letters and reports of Committee on Coordination of Entomology with the War Effort; report on Conference on War Problems in Biology and Agriculture; report on War Activities of Bureau of Entomology and Plant Quarantine; U.S. Dept. of Ag. Report on Ag. Production of 1943.
Correspondence with the following: P.N. Annand, Chief Bureau of Ent., Washington, D.C.; Robert Griggs; C.E. Palm; Dept. of Entomology, Cornell University; Harry B. Weiss, President Am. Assn. Econ. Ent.; F.L. Campbell; C.H. Richardson; Charles P. Alexander, Pres. Ent. Soc. of Am.; F.C. Bishop, Chief Joint Committee on War-Work Training; C.E. Mickel, Ent. Soc. of America; E.O. Essig, professor of Entomology, Univ. of California; John S. Hauser; W.B. Herms, Univ. of California; Ernest N. Cory; H.A. Wallace, Sec., Dept. of Ag.; H.B. Hungerford, Univ. of Kansas; Harold D. Smith, Dir., Bureau of Budget; Avery S. Hoyt, U.S. Dept. of Ag.Correspondence re honey grading - H.W. Samson, U.S. Bureau of Ag. Econ., E.P. Norgord, Dept. of Farms and Markets, Albany48Crop Protection Committee reports and correspondenceFeb. 13-14, 194349
Scope and Contents
Correspondence with E.C. Stackman, Chairman; Robert F. Griggs, Chairman Div. of Biol. and Ag., Natl. Research Council; Major Gen. Lewis B. Hershey; Dr. J.G. Leach, Acting Chairman, War Emer. Committee; Harry B. Weiss; J.D. Walker, Chairman of Sub-committee on Seed Certification; Ross G. Harrison, Chairman, Natl. Research Council. Also, correspondence with Dr. Walter F. Loehwing and G.W. Keitt, Prof. of Plant Pathology, Univ. of Wisconsin
Mimeo entitled "A Proposal re Ag. Services in Foreign Territories Occupied by American Troops."
Report on meeting of Am. Phytopathological Society, Feb. 13-14, 1943. Report of Fungicide Sub-committee, War Emergency Committee, Am. Phytopathological Society. Memo on the War Problem of Manpower in Plant PathologyCorrespondence, review, and contract on W.F. Phillips' book "Beekeeping." L.H. Bailey, H.S. Latham, The Macmillan Co., E.A. Elder410Lloyd R. Watson, data on variation in tongue length of progeny of queenbees1937411Correspondence re patent assignment of Dyce Process to Cornell University; use of royalties; honey prices; building of apiary; complaints of liscenses. Lake and biog. note on him413Coordination of entomology with war effort; occupational classification bulletin. Correspondence with Ernest N. Cory, A.G.
